channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How associations between historical redlining and breast cancer survival changed over time
item.description: A 1930s to 1960s residential segregation policy has been linked to shorter survival time in people with breast cancer. New research reveals that this ...

item.title: UK's growing synthetic opioid problem: Nitazene deaths could be underestimated by a third
item.description: The presence of nitazenes on the unregulated drug market has risen steeply in the last seven years—prompting UK and international bodies to issue publ...

item.title: Brief, intensive exercise may help patients with panic disorder more than standard care
item.description: Panic attacks are sudden bouts of intense fear without an obvious cause. An estimated 10% of people experience at least one panic attack in their life...

item.title: Decades of drinking reshape gene expression in key human brain regions, study shows
item.description: Chronic alcohol consumption profoundly alters gene expression in key brain regions involved in reward, impulse control, and decision-making, according...

item.title: How to 'green' operating rooms: New guideline advises reduce, reuse, recycle, and rethink
item.description: Reduce, reuse, recycle, and rethink can be applied in Canadian operating rooms (ORs) to increase environmental sustainability, advises a new guideline...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris challenges the conventional focus on sleep duration and makes the case that sleep timing and circadi...

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
item.description: Seed oils have become one of the most controversial topics in nutrition, especially after a 2024 University of South Florida study linked high omega-6...

item.title: RHR: The Longevity Trap: Why Chasing Anti-Aging May Be Aging You
item.description: In this episode, Chris explains why obsession with anti-aging hacks may actually undermine our long-term health. He examines the common longevity trap...
